1.5: The sons of Yaphet: Gamar and Magug and Maday and Yavan and Tubeil and Mashak and Tiras.
1.6: The sons of Gamar: Ashknez and Diphar and Tugarma.
1.7: And the sons of Yavan: Elisha and Tarshish and Katheem and Dudaneem.
1.8: And the sons of Kham: Cush and Metsraim and Phut and Canaan.
1.9: And the sons of Cush: Shebah and Khavila and Sabta and Rama and Sebaktha and the sons of Rama: Shebah and Daran.
1.10: And Cush begot Nemruud; he began to be a mighty man in the earth.
1.11: And Metsrim begot Ludeem and Yabeem, Lahbeem and Yaphtukheem.
1.12: And Pathruseem and Kaslukheem of whom had gone out from there the Philistines and Qapduqians.
1.13: And Canaan begot Tsidon his firstborn and the Khethites.
1.14: And the Yebusites and the Amorites and the Gergusites.
1.15: And the Kharites and the Arqites and the Sinites.
1.16: And the Arudites and the Tsamrites and the Khamathites.
1.17: The sons of Shaym: Ilam and Ashud and Arphakshar and Lud and Aram and Uz and Khul and Gathar and Mash.
1.18: And Arphakshar begot Shalakh and Shalakh begot Abar.
1.19: And to Abar were born two sons his name of one Palag because in his days was divided the Earth and the name of his brother Yaqtan.
1.20: And Yaqtan begot Elmudad and Shalaph and Khetsarmuth and Yarakh.
1.21: And Haduram and Uzel and Deqla.
1.22: And Ubal and Abimel and Shaba.
1.23: And Auphir and Akhwila and Yubab. All these are the sons of Yaqtan:
1.28: And these are sons of Abraham: Iskhaaq and Ishmaeyl.
1.29: And these are their generations: the firstborn of Ishmael: Nebiuth, also Qadar and Arbal and Mabsem.
1.30: And Mashmah and Ruma, Massa and Hadad, Taymna.
1.31: And Natur and Naphesh and Aqdama; these are the sons of Ishmaeyl.
1.32: And the sons of Qetura, the concubine of Abraham: Zamran and Yaqshan and Maran and Medyan Ashbaq and Ashwakh and the sons of Yaqshan: Shebah and Daran.
1.33: And the sons of Medyan: Epha and Aphar and ENoah and Abida and Elda; these all are sons of Qetura.
1.34: And Abraham begot Isaaq; the sons of Isaaq: Esau and Israel.
1.35: The sons of Esau: Eliphaz and Ruyel and Yaush and Ilaam and Qurakh.
1.36: And the sons of Eliphaz: Tayman and Umar and Tsuph and Gatham and Qanez and Tamna and Ameliq.
1.37: The sons of Ruil: Nakhath and Zarakh and Shama and Maza.
1.38: And the sons of Seir: Lutan and Shubal and Tsebun and Ana and Dooshan and Atsar and Dayshan.
1.39: And the sons of Lutan: Khurar and Humam and the sister of Lutan, Tamna.
1.40: And the sons of Shubal: Anwan and Mankhath and Ubal and Shaphar and Uyam and the sons of Tsabown: Ana and Anah.
1.41: And the sons of Anah: Dishun and the sons of Dishun: Khamran and Ashkan and Yathran and Karan.
1.42: The sons of Atsar: Kalhan and Zamun and Yaqan and the sons of Dishun: Uz and Aram.
1.43: And these are the Kings that reigned in the land of Edom, before a king would reign over the children of Israel: Bala, son of Beor, was made king in Edom, and the name of his city, Daihab.
1.44: And Bala died, and after him Yubab, son of Zarakh, from Butsar, became king.
1.45: And Yubab died, and Khusham from the land of the south became king after him.
1.46: And Khusham died, and Hadad, son of Badad, became king after him, who put the Midianites to the sword in the fields of the Moabites, and the name of his city, Gweeth.
1.47: And Hadad died, and after him Samla from Masreqa became king.
1.48: And Samla died, and after him Shaul from the River Rekhbuth became king.
1.49: And Shaul died, and Belkhnanen, son of Abkur, became king after him.
1.50: And Belkhnanen son of Abkur died, and after him Hadad became king, and the name of his city, Pau, and the name of his wife, Mahtwael, daughter of Matreed, son of Mizaheb; and Hadad died.
1.51: And there were Princes of Edom: Prince Tamna, Prince Ithith.
1.52: Prince Ahlibama, Prince Ilah, Prince Pinun.
1.53: Prince Qanez, Prince Tayman, Prince Mabtsar.
1.54: Prince Magdiyel, Prince Giram; these were the Princes of Edom.

2.1: And these are the sons of Israel: Rubil, Shemon, Levi, Yehuda, Issakar, Zebalaun
2.2: Dan, Yoseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, Ashir.
2.3: The sons of Yehuda: Eyr and Unaan and Shila; these three were born to him from Bathshua the Canaanitess, and Eyr was the firstborn of Yehuda; he was evil before LORD JEHOVAH, and he killed him.
2.4: And Tamar her daughter in law gave birth to Phares and Zarah; all the sons of Yehuda were five.
2.5: The sons of Pharets: Khetsrun and Khamuyel.
2.6: And the sons of Zerekh: Zamri and Athan and Haman and Kalcal and Darda, all five of them.
2.7: And the sons of Karmi Akar the impediment of Israel because he did evil with an accursed thing.
2.8: And the sons of Athan: Azaryah.
2.9: And the sons of Khetsrun who were born to him: Rakhmayel and Ram and Salki.
2.10: And Ram begot Aminadab and Aminadab begot Nakhshun, Prince of the children of Yehuda.
2.11: And Nakhshun begot Salma and Salma begot Baaz.
2.12: And Baaz begot Ubar and Ubar begot Iyshay.
2.13: And Iyshay begot his firstborn, Eliab, and the second, Abinadab and Shama the third.
2.14: And Nathanail the fourth and Dari the fifth.
2.15: And Utsum the sixth and Elihu the seventh and David the eighth.
2.16: And their two sisters Tsuria and Abigal; and the sons of Tsuria: Abishi and Yuab and Ashail, three.
2.17: And Abigal gave birth to Amasa, and the father of Amasa was Yathar.
2.18: And Kalab, son of Khetsron, begot from Aruba his wife, Idiuth, and these are her sons: Dakar, Ashir and Shubab and Aduun.
2.19: And Aruba died and Kalab took to himself Ephrath, and she bore to him Khur.
2.20: And Khur begot Uri and Uri begot Betsaliel.
2.21: And after these things, Khetsron entered into the daughter of Makir, the father of Gelad, and he took her and he was a son of sixty years, and she bore to him Sagub.
2.22: And Sagub begot Yair, and he had twentythree towns in the land of Gelad.
2.24: And after these things, Khetsron died in the land of Kalab with Ephrath, and she had given sons birth to him, Ashtwair the father of Taqu.
2.25: And the sons of Yarkhmel were: the firstborn of Khetsrun, Aram and Banna, and Aran and Atsam their sister.
2.26: And Yarkhmel had another wife, and her name was Etra; she was the mother of Eunim.
2.27: And the sons of Aram, the firstborn of Yarkhmel, were Maz and Nabin and Utar.
2.28: And the sons of Eunim were Sabi and Yuda, and the sons of Sabi: Nadab and Abishur.
2.29: And the name of the wife of Abishur was Abikhel, and she bore to him Akhban and Mulid.
2.30: And the sons of Nadab: Salad, Palarim, and Salad died without sons.
2.31: And the sons of Palarim: Eshaia and the sons of Eshaiah: Shushan, and the sons of Shushan, Akhli.
2.32: And the sons of Yuda, the brother of Sabi: Yathar and Yonathan, and Yathar died without sons.
2.33: And the sons of Yonathan: Lapath and Uza; these were the sons of Yarkhmel.
2.34: And Shushan had no male children, but only daughters, and Shushan had one man, one son in law from Matsra, and his name was Yardakha.
2.35: And had given him Shushan his daughter for a wife, and she bore to him, Atti.
2.36: And Atti begot Nathan, and Nathan begot Debir.
2.37: And Debir begot Aphil, and Aphil begot Yubab.
2.38: And Yubab begot Azraya, and Azraya begot Yahu, and Yahu begot Azraya.
2.39: And Azraya begot Khalaz, and Khalaz begot Elasa.
2.40: And Elasa begot Samsi, and Samsi begot Shalum.
2.41: And Shalum begot Elqamyah, and Elqamyah begot Elishma.
2.42: And the sons of Kalab, the brother of Irakhmel: Elishmi, the firstborn is the father of Ziph, and the sons of Marasha, the father of Khebroun:
2.43: Qurakh and Tappukh and Raqem and Shema.
2.44: And Shema begot Rakham, the father of Yarqam, and Yarqam begot Sammi.
2.45: And Uphna of the concubine of Kalab gave birth to Khuran, and Khuran begot Guzan.
2.46: These were the sons of Kalab, son of Khur, the firstborn of Aphrath: Shabal was born in Quryath Narin.
2.51: Samla was born in Bayth Lekhem; Abi was born in Gadar.
2.52: And the sons to Shabal in Quryath were Narin, Atruth, Yubal, Khatsri.
2.54: Sepharim, Netuphath, Samla, Shemuthim, Shekab, Khama.
2.55: All these were sons to Shabal in Quryath: Narin.

3.1: And these were the sons to David in Khebruun: the name of his firstborn son, Khamnun of Akhinam who was from Yizreil, the second, Kalab, from Abigel the wife of Nabal who was from Mount Karmela.
3.2: The third, Abishlum, son of Maaka, daughter of Talmay, King of Geshur, the fourth, Aduniah son of Khagith.
3.3: The fifth, Shephatiah, son of Aphietel, the sixth, Ituram, who was from Egla, the wife of David.
3.4: These six sons were born to David in Khebruun, and he reigned there seven years and six months, and thirty and three years he reigned in Jerusalem.
3.5: And these are the names of the sons who were born to him in Jerusalem: Shemua and Shakub and Nathan and Solomon.
3.6: And Yabkhar and Elishema and Elida.
3.7: And Eliphlat and Nugah and Nephag.
3.9: These are all the sons of David, and Tamar was their sister.
3.10: And the sons of Solomon: Rekhabaam his son, Abia the son of Asa, the son of Yushaphat his son.
3.11: Yuram his son, Ekhazyah his son, Yoash his son.
3.12: Amutsia his son, Uzzia his son, Yotham his son.
3.13: Akhaz his son, Hezekiah his son, Manassheh his son.
3.14: Amon his son, Yoshiah his son.
3.15: And the sons of Yoshia: the firstborn Yokhanan, the second Yoiqim, the third Tsedeqia, the fourth Shallum.
3.16: And the sons of Yoiqim: Yokania his son, Tsedeqia his son.
3.17: And the son of Yokania, Ashrashtil his son.
3.18: And Malcum his son and Peraia his son and Shaatsar his son and Neqamia his son and Shemua his son and Nedabia his son.
3.19: And the sons of Nedabia: Zurbabeil and Shami, and the sons of Zurbabeil: Meshlam and KhananYah and Selqath their sister.
3.20: And Kheshiba and Yahuyel and Bria and Khesadya.
3.21: The sons of KhananYah: Pelatyah and Ushaya, Arphaya his son, Arnon his son, Oubedaya the son of Ushaya.
3.22: And the son of Shekania: Shemaiah; and the son of Shemaiah, Khatush; and Negayel and Azarya and Khezaqaya.
3.24: And Hudiah and Alshib and Pelayah and Yaqub and Yokhanan and Daliah and Anen.

4.1: And these are the sons of Yehuda: Pharez and Khetsrun and Karmi and Yanur and Shubal.
4.2: And Laiah, son of Shubal, begot Nakhath, and Nakhath begot Akhimi and Alyad; these are the generations of Rekhabam.
4.3: And these the sons of Aminadab: Akhizarel and Neshma and Dibash.
4.4: And Peguyel and Khushiya, and these are the sons of Khur, the firstborn of Aphrath, whose father was from Bayth Lekhem.
4.5: And Akhishur, the father of Tequ, had two wives.
4.6: And one of them bore Akhiram and Taphar and Tayman and Kharshtar; these were the children of one of them.
4.9: And one of them was precious to his mother and to his father, and he called his name Aynay.
4.10: And he said to him: “Blessing, LORD JEHOVAH will bless you, and he shall multiply your border, and his hand shall be with you, and he shall save you from the Evil One that he will not have power over you, and he will give you whatever you ask him.”
4.11: And Kalab the brother of Akhia begot Mekhir; he was the father of Eshtun.
4.12: And Eshtun begot Rupha, and Rupha begot Paskhi, and Paskhi begot Tekhia, and Tekhia begot Yazir.
4.15: These were the sons of Kalab, son of Yuphna: the name of his firstborn son, Ala, and the name of the second, Naam, and the name of the third, Qanz, and the name of the fourth, Ashiph, and the name of the fifth, Yamuyel, and the name of the sixth, Yahrub, these were sons of Kalab, son of Yuphna.
4.19: And the sons of the wife of Uriah, the sister of Nakhum, the father of Qayla: Zemri and Eshtmu and Makith and Eshtma and Ashimun.
4.20: And the sons of Ashimun: Amon and Dumia and Zarakh and Shela.
4.24: These are the sons of Yehuda: Yamueil and Yamin and Ahar and Yakeen and Tsakhar, and Reeb, and Zarakh, and Shaul, the brother of Nakhum, the father of Qayla.
4.25: And the sons of Shela, the son of Yehuda.
4.26: And the sons of Shemon: Mebsam his son, Mashma his son, Shemathi his son, Khemel his son, Zakki his son, Shami his son.
4.27: And Shami had sixteen sons and six daughters, and his brother did not have many children, and all their families did not increase until the children of Yehuda came to him.
4.28: And they dwelt with them in the towns of Beer Sheba, and in Mulada, and in Darath Shua
4.32: And in Alha and in Etsrem and in Elathlad.
4.33: And in Ansel and in Kherma and in Khatsragada and in Kheshmun and in Khethplet and in Tsnaqlag
4.34: And in Marmarana and Samsala, these towns until King David, and their villages.
4.35: And Aqim and Aqin and Remun and Athken and Ashen, five towns.
4.36: And all the hamlets that were around these towns of the Arqites, these were the towns of their habitation, and they had the great name of their beautiful habitation, and tranquility and peace was around them.
4.38: And these are the names of the Princes who were there with their families, and in the house of their fathers.
4.39: And they came to the entrance of Gadar unto the east of the valley to seek pasture for their sheep.
4.40: And they found pasture that was good and lush, and land great and good, and tranquility and peace was there because of those dwelling in it from the first.
4.41: And these men who were written in the years of Hezekiah, King of the house of Yehuda, came and they destroyed their tents, and they polluted every spring of waters that was there until today, and they dwelt in their places, because the pasture of their sheep was very good.
4.42: And some of the sons of Shemon went to the Mount of Gabel, five hundred men, and these are the names of the men who went to their front: Pelatiah and Matitha and Rephaia and Uziel; these four men, the sons of Ishi, went to their front.
4.43: And they attacked with the sword all the rest of the Amalekites who were left, and they have dwelt in their place until today.

5.1: And the sons of Rubil, the firstborn of Israel, because he was the firstborn of his father and he despised the bed of his father, his birthright was given to Yoseph his brother, son of Israel, and upon these two came a blessing from all the tribes of Israel.
5.2: From Yehuda will come forth The King Messiah , and the right of the firstborn shall be given to Yoseph. .
5.3: The sons of Rubil, the firstborn of Israel: Khenuk and Palu and Khetsrun and Karmi.
5.4: And the sons of Karmi: Shemaiah his son, Duag his son, Shemei his son
5.5: Mikah his son, Uriah his son, Bela his son
5.6: Abdiah his son, whom TeglathPalasar, King of Assyria, took captive. He was the Prince of the tribe of the house of Rubil,
5.7: And his brothers with their families, and when they were written by genealogy in their lineage, their Chief Azrayl, the second, Zechariah
5.8: And Bela, son of Uzi, son of Shemei, son of Yoel, was dwelling in Aduir, and unto Nebu and to the Plain of Maun.
5.9: And to its East unto the borders of the wilderness that is from the river Euphrates, because they and their possessions increased in the land of Gelad.
5.10: And in the days of Shaul they made war with Arabians dwellers of Saka, and they were given into their hands, and they dwelt in their tents on all the face of the east of the land of Gelad.
5.11: And the children of Gad dwelt opposite them in the land of Mathnin, unto the border of Siba and of Salka.
5.12: And Yoel went out at their front, and he was judging them and he taught them beautiful scriptures.
5.14: These are the sons of Abaykhel, son of Khur, son of Zarakh, son of Gelad, son of Makir
5.15: Son of Abdeil, son of Eli, Chiefs of their fathers.
5.16: And they dwelt in Mathnin and in its villages.
5.17: All of them were written by genealogy in the days of Yotham, King of the house of Yehuda, and in the days of Yorbaam, King of Israel.
5.18: And the sons of Rubil and Gad and half the tribe of Menashe, men holding a sword and shield, and shooters of the bow and acquainted with war, forty and four thousand and six hundred and sixty and six, these all went out into war.
5.19: And they made war with the dwellers of Saka.
5.20: And they were delivered into their hands, because they had prayed to LORD JEHOVAH in battle, and he paid attention to their voice, because they trusted upon him.
5.21: And they plundered many riches and property and fifty thousand camels and two hundred and fifty thousand sheep and two thousand male donkeys, and a hundred thousand souls of men.
5.22: Because many fell slain from their tents in battle, and they remained in their places by their tents.
5.23: And the children of half of the tribe of Menashe dwelt in the land of Mathnin unto the valley of Mount Hermon and unto Sair, the great Mountain of Hermon, they increased unto HarTeman, and they became great.
5.24: And these were the heads of the house of their fathers: Apha and Shub and Eldaa and Azrayel and Adumya and Uriyah and Khezayel, mighty men of the army, men of fame, for they were the heads of the house of their fathers.
5.25: And they denied the God of their fathers, and they went astray after gods of the nations of the Earth whom LORD JEHOVAH had destroyed from before them.
5.26: And LORD JEHOVAH God of Israel aroused the spirit of TeglathPalasar King of Assyria against them, and he led the tribe of the house of Rubil captive, and the tribe of the house of Gad, and the half tribe of Menashe, and he brought them to Khalakh and to Khabar the River, Guzan, cities of Media, and they dwell there until today.

6.1: The sons of Levi: Gershun and Qahath and Merari.
6.2: And the children of Qahath: Amram and Itshar and Khebron and Uzeil.
6.3: And the children of Amram: Moshe and Aaron and Maryam, and the children of Aaron: Nadab and Abihu and Eliazar and Ithamar.
6.4: And Eliazar begot Phinkhes, and Phinkhes begot Abishu.
6.5: And Abishu begot Abidar, and Abidar begot Azi.
6.6: And Azi begot Zerakhyah, and Zerakhyah begot Maru.
6.7: And Maru begot Amaryah, and Amaryah begot Akhitub.
6.8: And Akhitub begot Tsaduq, and Tsaduq begot Akhimets.
6.9: And Akhimets begot Azrayah, and Azrayah begot Yukhanan.
6.10: And Yukhanan begot Azaryah, and Azaryah ministered in the house that Solomon built in Jerusalem.
6.11: And Azaryah begot Amaryah, and Amaryah begot Akhitub.
6.12: And Akhitub begot Tsadoq, and Tsaduq begot Shalum.
6.13: And Shalum begot Khelaqyah, and Khelaqyah begot Azaryah.
6.14: And Azaryah begot Serayah, and Serayah begot Tsaduq.
6.15: And Tsaduq was taken captive when LORD JEHOVAH led the men of Yehuda and Jerusalem captive to Babel, to Nebukadnetsar.
6.16: The sons of Levi: Gershun and Qahath and Merari.
6.17: And these are names of the sons of Gershun: Lebni and Shemi.
6.18: And the sons of Qahath: Amram and Yatshar and Khebrun and Uziel.
6.19: And the sons of Marari: Makhli and Mushi; these are the generations of Levi for their fathers:
6.20: Gershun, his son Nakhath, his son Zema his son
6.21: Yuwakh his son, Adu his son, Yatur his son
6.22: The sons of Qahath: Aminadab his son, Qurakh his son, Asir his son
6.23: Halqanah his son, Akensaph his son
6.24: Takhuth his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, Shaul his son.
6.25: And the sons of Halqana: Amshi and Akhimuth
6.26: Halqanah his son, Tsuph his son, Nakhath his son, Zarakh his son, Shemala his son, Mari his son
6.27: Eliab his son, Gadkhiel his son, Halqanah his son.
6.28: And the sons of Shamuyel: his firstborn son, Yuyel, and the name of his second son, Abia.
6.29: And the sons of Merari: Makhli his son, Lebni his son
6.30: Shemi his son, Aza his son, Shemaiah his son, Eshaia his son.
6.31: And these are all those whom David appointed that they would minister in the house of LORD JEHOVAH in the place where the Ark is set
6.32: And they would be serving before the Tabernacle in the Tent of Time, with hymns of great praise, until Solomon builds the house of LORD JEHOVAH which is in Jerusalem, and he appointed them to their occupations, according to their law.
6.33: And these and their sons of the sons of Qahath from the tribe of the Levites ministered: Haman and Yoel, son of Shemueil
6.34: Son of Halqanah, son of Yarkhum, son of Eliel, son of Takhu
6.35: The son of Tsuph, son of Halqanah, son of Khamath, son of Mushi
6.36: Son of Halqanah, son of Yuel, son of Azaryah, son of Zephnaya
6.37: Son of Takhath, son of Asir, son of Aksiph, son of Qurakh
6.38: Son of Yatshar, son of Qahath, son of Levi, son of Israel
6.39: And his brother Asaph who stood at the right of Asaph, son of Brakyah, son of Shemaiah.
6.40: Son of Mikayel, son of Masya, son of Melakya
6.41: Son of Athni, son of Zarakh, son of Araya
6.42: Son of Athan, son of Zema, son of Shemi
6.43: Son of Yukha, son of Gershun, son of Levi
6.44: The sons of Merari their brother who stood at the left side: Athan, son of Qishi, son of Amar, son of Maluk
6.45: Son of Kheshabya, son of Amutsaya, son of Khelaqya
6.47: Son of Makhli, son of Mushi, son of Merari, son of Levi.
6.48: And their brothers the Levites who were giving to all the works of the house of the Tabernacle of the house of LORD JEHOVAH.
6.49: And Aaron and his sons offered sacrifices on the altar, and upon the altar of incense burners of sweet spices, all the work of the Holy of Holies, to make atonement for Israel as Moshe the Servant of LORD JEHOVAH commanded.
6.50: And these are the sons of Aaron: Eliazar his son, Phinkhes his son, Abishu his son
6.51: Baqi his son, Azi his son, Zerakhyah his son
6.52: Maru his son, Amaryah his son, Akhitub his son
6.53: Tsaduq his son, Akhimats his son.
6.54: And these are the names of the cities that came to the families in their borders: to the children of Aaron, the family of Qahath, because to them the first lot arrived.
6.55: And they gave them Khebruun in the land of Yehuda and all the hamlets that are around it that are in the land of Yehuda.
6.56: And all the fields of the cities that were around it they gave to Kalab, son of Yuphna.
6.57: And to the children of Aaron they gave all the cities of salvation. They gave to the Priests Khebron and its hamlets, and Lebna and its hamlets, and Yathar and its hamlets, and Lekhem and its hamlets, and Eshtemu and its hamlets
6.59: And Ashan and its hamlets, and Etra and its hamlets, and Bayth Shemesh and its hamlets.
6.60: And from the tribe of Benyamin they gave Gebah and its hamlets, Almuth and its hamlets, Nathuth and its hamlets, all the cities were thirteen for their families.
6.61: And to the children of Qahath who inherited from the tribe of the family of Menashe, ten cities in number.
6.62: And for the children of Gershun for their families, from the tribe of Issakar, and from the tribe of the house of Ashir, and from the tribe of Menashe, and from the tribe of the house of Naphtali, thirteen cities in number.
6.63: To the children of Marari for their families, from the tribe of the house of Rubil, and from the tribe of the children of Gad, and from the tribe of the children of Zabalaun, thirteen cities in number.
6.64: And the children of Israel gave the Levites these cities and their hamlets.
6.65: And they gave by lots from the tribe of the house of Yehuda, and from the tribe of the children of Shemon, and from the tribe of Benyamin these cities that were themselves called by the name of the families of the children of Qahath.
6.66: And the cities and their borders were from the tribe of the house of Aphreim.
6.67: And they gave them the cities of salvation: Shecheem and its villages in the mountain of the tribe of the house of Aphreim, and Yazir and its hamlets
6.68: And Bayth Khuran and its hamlets
6.69: And Alilun and its hamlets, and Gethremun and its hamlets.
6.70: And from half of the tribe of Menashe they gave Anath and its hamlets, and Yablin and its hamlets, and to the tribe of the children of Qahath that they inherited.
6.71: To the children of Gershun from half of the tribe of Menashe: Gulan that is in Mathnin and its hamlets and Ashtur and its hamlets.
6.72: And from the tribe of Issakar: Raqem and its hamlets, Dabruth and its hamlets
6.74: And from the tribe of the house of Ashir: Mashal and its hamlets, Ebrun and its hamlets
6.75: Qiq and its hamlets, Dahab and its hamlets.
6.76: And from the tribe of the house of Naphtali: Reqem that is in Galila and its hamlets, Hamun and its hamlets, Quryathim and its hamlets.
6.77: And from the tribe of the house of Merari that they inherited from the tribe of the house of Zabalaun: Armuni and its hamlets, Tabor and its hamlets.
6.78: And at the crossing of Jordan of Yerikho, from the east of the Jordan, from the tribe of the house of Rubil: Butsar in the wilderness and its hamlets, Mepuphath and its hamlets, Yahats and its hamlets
6.79: Qadmuth and its hamlets, Muphath and its hamlets
6.80: Ramuth and its hamlets, Makhlam and its hamlets
6.81: Yezir and its hamlets, Kheshbon and its hamlets.

7.1: The sons of Issakar: Tula and Phua and Shuub and Shemroon, these his four sons.
7.2: The sons of Tula: Azi, Arphaya, Yuyel, Lakhmi and Yabsam and Shamuyel, the Chiefs among their fathers. These are the sons of Tula, mighty men of power for their families in their generations by their number, and in the days of David, twenty and two thousand and six hundred.
7.3: And the sons of Uuz: Izarkhan; and the sons of Izarkhan: Malkayel and Oubdaya and Yuel and Ishwa, four Chiefs, all of them.
7.4: Their families for the house of their fathers, mighty men of the army and makers of war, thirty and six thousand, because they increased, and their wives and their children.
7.5: And their brothers, all the families of the house of Issakar, mighty men of the army: eighty and seven thousand.
7.6: And these are the names of the sons of Benjamin: Bela and Akbar and Ashkuul and Gaara and Naaman and Akhi and Aruush and Maphi and Khapheem and Adar.
7.7: And the sons of Baala: Atsbuun and Azi and Zayel and Yarmuth and Azri five Chiefs of the house of their father, mighty men of the army, and the number of them was twenty and two thousand and thirty and four.
7.8: And the sons of Akbar: Zemura and Yawush and Eliazar and Elani and Amri and Yarmuth and Abia and Anathuth and Almuth; these all are the sons of Akbar.
7.9: And their number was for their families, for the Chiefs among their fathers to their generations, mighty men of the army, twenty and two thousand and two hundred.
7.10: And these are the sons of Ashkul: Balhan; and the sons of Balhan: Yawush and Benjamin and Ihur and the Canaanites, and Zaytuun and Tarshish and Akheshrakh.
7.11: All these are the sons of Ashkul, to the heads of their fathers for their families, mighty men of the army, seventeen thousand and two hundred who go out in the army of war.
7.12: And Shaphan and Khaphis, the sons of Gur, the sons of Khusha.
7.13: The sons of Naphtali: Yatshayel and Guni and Yatsar and Shalom, these are the sons of Balha.
7.14: The sons of Menashe whom his concubine gave birth to him: Isarkel and Aduniah, and she bore Mekir the father of Gelad.
7.15: And Makir took to him a wife, one daughter of a Prince, and the name of his sister was Maaka, and the name of his elder brother was Tsalphkhad, and Tsalphkhad had no sons, only daughters.
7.16: Also Maaka mother of Makir bore a son, and she called his name Peresh, and the name of his brother Sheresh, and the sons of Alum: Reqem.
7.17: And the sons of Reqem: Baran. These are the sons of Gelad, son of Makir, son of Manassheh.
7.18: And his sister Maaka gave birth to Ashkhur and Abiazar
7.19: And Shemira, and Alinun, and Shim and Etham.
7.20: And the sons of Aphreim: Shuthelakh, and Bakar his son, and Takhath his son, and Eldaa his son and Akhath his son
7.21: And Zabur his son, and Shultakh his son, and Lazar his son, the men, the sons of Gath who were born in the land. They had gone down to take their wealth.
7.22: And Aphreim their father was mourning many days, and his brothers came to comfort him and to put consolation in his heart.
7.23: And he came to unity with his wife, and she conceived and she bore a son, and she called his name Bria, because there was evil with him in his house
7.24: And his daughter who remained in Bayth Khuran, lower and upper.
7.25: And his daughter healed all these who remained, because she was a Nurse and she healed their sick.
7.26: And she healed Adan the son of Amihud.
7.27: Of the sons of Anun the father of Yehushba.
7.28: And their property and their beds in Bayth Eil and its hamlets, and Shekeem and its hamlets, and Anath and its hamlets.
7.29: And upon the borders of the sons of Manassheh: Bayth Shekeem and its hamlets, Tanak and its hamlets, Magdu and its hamlets, Dur and its hamlets, as these towns dwelt the children of Yoseph son of Israel.
7.30: The sons of Ashayr: Yamna and Shua and Shwi and Braya and Sarakh their sister.
7.31: And the sons of Bria: Khabar and Malkil, he who is the father of Barzith.
7.32: And Khaphar begot Phelet and Shamir and Khatum and Shu their sister.
7.33: And the sons of Phelet: Pasak and Bemhal and Ashut, and these are the sons of Palet.
7.40: These all are the sons of Ashir, Heads of the house of their fathers in their generations, mighty men of the army, Chiefs of the Princes, for their accounting in the army of war was by their number, twenty and six thousand men.

8.1: And Benjamin begot Baala his firstborn, Akbar the second, and Ashkul the third
8.2: And Gera the fourth, and Naman the fifth, and Akhi the sixth, and Aruush the seventh, and Mathim the eighth, and Khasim the ninth and Adar the tenth.
8.3: And sons to Baala were Abdu and Gera and Abihur
8.4: And Abishu and Naman and Akhia
8.5: And Shaphim and Khupham and Akhiram.
8.6: These are the sons of Abihur, and these were the Chiefs of their fathers for their families, and they were taken captive to the plain of Naaman.
8.9: And he begot from Kharash his wife, Yubab and Tsabya and Malkum.
8.10: And Menasheh and Sariyah and Yarmana and Tsabats, these are his sons, Chiefs of the fathers.
8.12: And the sons of Eliphleg: Ubir and Mashlam and Shamir, and he built Iyu and Lud and its villages.
8.13: And he was the head of the fathers over the dwellers of Gath.
8.15: And Zebadya and Azur and Adi.
8.16: And Mankel and Ansheephi and Ebrun and Zabdi.
8.24: And Khaneni and KhanenYah and Ulam and Anathuth.
8.25: And Peraya and Phael sons of Shishaq and Shemira.
8.28: These are Heads of the fathers for their generations, these of those first dwelling in Jerusalem.
8.29: And the father of Gebuun was dwelling in Gebuun, and the name of his wife, Maaka.
8.30: And his firstborn son, Ebrun, and Qish, and Bala and Nadab
8.31: And Gud, and Akhib, and Izabar, and Maqluth.
8.32: And Maqluth begot a hundred, also those were dwelling next to their brethren in Jerusalem.
8.33: And Nir begot Qish and Qish begot Shaul and Shaul begot Jonathan and Melkishua and Yashui and Eshbashul.
8.34: And the son of Yonathan was crippled in his feet, and Yashui had a son Merabel, and Merabel begot Mika.
8.35: And the sons of Mica: Pethyun and Melek and Tara and Akhur and Aran.
8.36: And Merabel begot Yahuyada, and Yahuyada begot Almuth and Azmuth and Zamri, and Zamri begot Mutsiya.
8.37: And Mutsiya begot Keneniya, and Keneniya begot Zupha, and Elassa his son, Atsal his son.
8.38: And Atsal had six sons: Azri his firstborn, Qim his second, Ishmael the third, and Shadya and Oubadya and Khanan, these all were the sons of Atsel.
8.39: And the sons of Ashiq, his brother: Ulam his firstborn, Yaush the second, and Eliphlat the third.
8.40: The sons of Alum were mighty men of the army and holders of the bow, and they were teaching their sons and their grandsons, a hundred and fifty, all these from the tribe of the children of Benjamin.

9.1: And all the children of Israel and the children of Yehuda were considered evil, those who were taken captive to Babel in their evil.
9.2: And those who were first in their inheritance and in their cities: Israel and the Priests and the Levites and the Proselytes.
9.3: And some of the children of Yehuda were dwelling in Jerusalem, and some of the children of Benjamin, and some of the children of Aphreim and some of the children of Manassheh
9.4: Zuri son of Amihud, son of Aemri, son of Amri, son of Benjamin, the sons of Pharez, the sons of Yehuda.
9.5: From the place of the Shilunites: Eshaia the firstborn and Batsya his brother.
9.6: And from the sons of Zarakh: Yawayel and their brothers, six hundred and ninety.
9.7: And of the sons of Benjamin: Selu, son of Mashlam, son of Hudia, son of Yahbana
9.8: And Yukanya, son of Yarkhum. These are the sons of Azi, son of Makir, and Mashlam, son of Arubel, son of Ikanya.
9.9: And their brothers for their families, nine hundred and ninety and nine, all these mighty men of the army, Chiefs of hundreds for their families.
9.10: And of the Priests: Yunadab, Yuda, Zakim
9.11: Azarayah, son of Khelqia, son of Mashlam, son of Tsaduq, son of Mazu, son of Akhitub, whose house was situated next to the house of LORD JEHOVAH.
9.12: And Azraya, son of Yarkhum, son of Pashkhur, son of Melakya, and Mansi, son of Udyayel, son of Yukhanan, son of Mashlam, son of Mashdawuth, son of Amar.
9.13: And their brothers were Heads of the house of their fathers, one thousand and seven hundred and sixty mighty men of the army, workers of the work of the house of LORD JEHOVAH.
9.14: And from the Levites: Shemaiah, son of Kheshum, son of Zarqim, son of Kheshabya.
9.15: From the sons of Merari: Yarkhum, son of Khadush, son of Alal, Methanya, son of Miqa, son of Zabdi, son of Asaph.
9.16: And Oubdaya, son of Shemaiah, son of Kalal, son of Yirithun, and Berakya, son of Asaph, son of Halqanah, who was dwelling in Ramtha
9.17: Shalum, and Yaqub, and Talan, and Khamnun, and Akhihum, and Shalum.
9.18: Thus far is the eastern gate of the King. These were the Doorkeepers who stand by the Guards of the children of Levi.
9.19: And Shalum, son of Qura, son of Aksiph, son of Qurakh, and his brothers and the men of the household of his father who were appointed over the work, and the Guards of the exit and the entrance.
9.20: And Phinkhas, son of Eliazar, was Master over them from the first, and LORD JEHOVAH was with him.
9.21: Zechariah, son of Mashlam guarded the door of the Tent of Time.
9.22: All these stood at the door, in number, two hundred and twelve; these were counted in their generations, these whom David and Samuel the Prophet put into their office.
9.23: And they appointed them and their children that they would be to keep these doors of the house of LORD JEHOVAH and the house of the Time Tabernacle and the watch garrisons.
9.24: To the four winds they were opening these gates, to the East and to the West and to the North and the South.
9.25: And their brothers were keeping these in their generations and were not entering except once every seven days, from time to time.
9.26: Because those were in the office, because these Levites were standing and guarding over the four winds of the gates, and those Rulers were over the work and over the treasury of the house of LORD JEHOVAH.
9.27: And they were going around and lodging at the house of LORD JEHOVAH, because this was their Guard at the gates.
9.28: And at every dawn they were inspecting all the implements of service, because they were bringing them in by number, and by number they were taking them out.
9.29: And some of the Levites were authorized over the implements and over all the work of Holiness, over the altar, and over the vessels, and over the wine, and over the oil, and over the frankincense and over the pure sweet spices.
9.30: And some sons of the Priests prepared sweet spices for the censer.
9.31: And a gift from the Levites was given, and Matitha of the Levites was the firstborn of Shalum; he was authorized in the office over secret services.
9.32: Also some of the sons of Qahath were authorized over their brethren and over the bread of the curtains to prepare a feast on the Sabbath.
9.33: And these Ministers, Chiefs of the fathers of Levi, were guarding in the arched chambers that are around the house, because they were authorized over the work by day and by night.
9.34: And these were the Chiefs of the fathers of Levi in their families, and they were dwelling in Jerusalem.
9.35: And the father of Gebuun was dwelling in Gebuun, and the name of his firstborn son: Joel, and the name of his wife: Maaka.
9.36: And his second son: Ebron, also Tsur, and Qishun, and Bala, and Nir and Nadab
9.37: And Gedoud, and Akhia, and Zechariah and Maqluth.
9.38: And Maqluth begot Shamets; they also were dwelling next to their brothers, with their brothers in Jerusalem.
9.39: And Nir begot Qish, and Qish begot Shaul, and Shaul begot Jonathan, and Melkishua, and Yashwi and Eshbashul.
9.40: And Yashwi had a son and his name: Merabel, and Merabel begot Mika.
9.41: And the children of Mica: Pithun and Imlek and Akhaz.
9.42: And Akhaz begot Yazanya, and Yazanya begot Almuth, and Almuth begot Zamri and Zamri begot Moutsa.
9.43: And Moutsa begot Kanaaniya, Arphiya his son, Atsal his son, Elasa his son.
9.44: And Atsal had six sons, and these are their names: Azi, his firstborn, Qim his second, Ashma his son, Shadya his son, Oubadya his son, Khanan his son; these are the sons of Atsel.

10.1: And the Philistines were fighting with Israel, and the men of Israel fled from the Philistines, and many fell slain on the Mountain of Gelbua.
10.2: And the Philistines overtook Shaul and his sons, and the Philistines attacked Jonathan and Yashwi and Melkishua, the sons of Shaul, with the sword.
10.3: And the battle prevailed against Shaul, and the men archers who are very skillful to shoot with a bow found him, and when Shaul saw them, he was very afraid of their presence.
10.4: And Shaul said to his armor Bearer: “Draw out your sword and run me through with it before these uncircumcised will come and kill me, and they would perform torture on me!” And his Armor Bearer was unwilling because he was very afraid, and Shaul drew his sword and he fell upon it.
10.5: And his Armor Bearer saw for himself that Shaul was dead, and he fell also on his sword and died with him.
10.6: And Shaul died and his three sons, and his Armor Bearer, and all his mighty men died together on that day.
10.7: And every man of Israel who was at the crossing of the plain and he who was at the crossing of Jordan saw for himself where the people of Israel fled, and that Shaul and his sons were killed, and they left the cities and fled, and the Philistines came and they dwelt in them.
10.8: And it happened after daylight that the Philistines came to plunder the slain, and they found Shaul and his three sons killed, and they lay in the Mountain of Gelbua.
10.9: And they picked up their heads and they stripped their armor and they sent to the land of the Philistines to the towns and to the cities and to the surroundings to proclaim the good news in the houses of their idols and among their people.
10.10: And they placed their garments and their weapons in the houses of their idols, and their bodies they hung on the wall of Bayth Yashan.
10.11: And the inhabitants of Yabish of Gelad heard the thing that the Philistines had done to Shaul.
10.12: And every valiant man arose and they went all night and they took the body of Shaul and the body of his sons from the wall of Bayth Yashan, and they brought them to Yabish and burned them there, and they took their bones they buried them under the Oak which is at Yabish, and they fasted seven days.
10.13: And Shaul died because he lied to LORD JEHOVAH, and he did not keep the thing that he commanded him.
10.14: Also he inquired of the spirits of the dead, and he did not inquire of LORD JEHOVAH his God, and his kingdom was given to David, son of Iyshay.

11.1: And all the children of Israel assembled to David at Khebron, and they were saying to him: “We are your blood and your flesh!
11.2: Also yesterday and the day before yesterday when Shaul was King over us, you had been going out and coming in at the head of Israel, and LORD JEHOVAH said to you, ‘You go shepherd my people Israel, and you will be Leader over all the tribes of Israel.’”
11.3: And all the tribes of Israel came to the King at Khebrun, and King David swore an oath to them in Khebruun before LORD JEHOVAH, and they appointed David to be King over Israel, and the words of Samuel the Prophet that he had spoken in the name of LORD JEHOVAH were confirmed.
11.4: And David and all the children of Israel went on to Jerusalem, which was called Yabus from the first, and the Yabusites were the inhabitants of the land.
11.5: And the men, the dwellers of Yabus, were saying to David: “You shall not enter here!” And David gathered all the people, and he conquered those cities of Zion; these are called the cities of David.
11.6: Then David said: “Anyone who will first kill a Yebusite man, he will be Chief and General of the army!” And Yuab, son of Tsuria, came up first, and King David presented him as Chief and General of the army.
11.7: And David dwelt in the towns of Zion, because of this, they were calling them the towns of David.
11.8: And David built around it the city of freedom which is outside, and David gave the right side to the rest of the children of men who were in the villages.
11.9: And David was going on, and he became rich, and LORD JEHOVAH of Hosts was with him.
11.10: And these are the Chiefs of the men of David who were mighty. They were with him in his kingdom to establish him King over all Israel, according to the word of LORD JEHOVAH which he spoke about Israel.
11.11: And these are the numbers of the men of David: he who sat in the first seat was Chief of thirty, Gedkhu was a mighty man; he drew his spear and he killed three hundred men in one hour.
11.12: After him was Eliazar, son of his paternal uncle from the city Dakhukh; he was over three hundred men.
11.13: When he was with David in Pasidemaya and the Philistines were making war there, and there was one field that was sown with barley, and the people were scattered from before the Philistines.
11.14: And they stood within the field and they delivered it, and attacked the Philistines with the sword, and LORD JEHOVAH performed a great victory.
11.15: And three of the men went down to David to the cave of Arlam, and the camp of the Philistines were camping in the Valley of the Giants.
11.16: And David was dwelling in a tent, and the men of the Philistines in Bayth Lekhem.
11.17: And David lusted and said: “Who gives me water that I may drank from the great well of Bayth Lekhem that is at the gate of the city?”
11.18: And at that moment the three men heard, and they tore through and they entered into the camp of the Philistines, and they went and filled up waters from the great well that is in Bayth Lekhem, in the gate of the city, and they took and they brought and they gave to David, and he chose not to drink them and he poured them out before LORD JEHOVAH.
11.19: And he said: “God forbid me from LORD JEHOVAH that I would do this before my God! These men went out, they went by the blood of their throats!” And he was not willing to drink them. These things the three men did.
11.20: And Abishi the brother of Yoab was Chief of thirty, and he drew a lance against three hundred killed, and he had honor over the thirty.
11.21: And he was more honored than the thirty, and he was Chief over them, and was doing battle like thirty men.
11.22: Benaiah, son of Yawida, was a mighty man of power whose deeds were beautiful, from the fortress cities of Qabtsayel. He slew two mighty men of Moab, and he went down and he killed a lion within a forest on a day of snow.
11.23: And he killed a man, a mighty Egyptian man, whose height was five cubits, and in the hand of the Egyptian there was a lance whose point was thick as the beam of a weaver, and he went down upon him with a rod, and he took the spear from the hand of the Egyptian, and he killed him with the spear.
11.24: Benaiah son of Yawida did all these things, and he had a name greater than the three men
11.25: For he was honored more than the three, and he was doing battle like the three, and David appointed him that he would be the Keeper over his spear and over the mighty men of the army.
11.26: Shayel, the brother of Yuab, Elkhanan, son of his paternal uncle from Bayth Lekhem
11.27: Shemuth from the Mount of the King, Khalats who is from Paltaun
11.28: Ira, son of Eqis, who is from Teqoa, Abiazar, who is from Nathoth.
11.29: Sabi who is from Kheshubath, Ali who is from Khukh.
11.30: Mahri, who is from Netuphath, Khalad, son of Baana, who is from Netuphath.
11.31: Athi, son of Rabai, who is from Gebatha of the sons of Benjamin, Benaiah, son of Prathun.
11.32: Khadi who is from Nekhelgish, Abiel, son of Erabthun.
11.33: Uzban who is from Markham, Elipha who is from Shalbam.
11.34: The sons of Shaym who is from Azun, Jonathan son of Shaga who is from Mount Karmela.
11.35: Akhiram, son of Sakam, who is from the Mount of Baytha, Eliphun, son of Ur who is from Begarthun.
11.36: Khaphar, who is from Mekarthim, Akhiah who is from Khatsar.
11.37: Khetsri, who is from Karmela, Laazar, who is from Ubai.
11.38: Yuel, the brother of Yonathan, Mekkhad from Gad.
11.39: Tsalaq, an Ammonite, Makhzi, the Berathite, the Armor Bearer of the weapons of Yoab, son of Tsuria.
11.40: Ira, who is from Yathir, Arab who is from Yather.
11.41: Uriah the Khithite, Edinu, son of Shara.
11.42: Edinu, son of Shara, from the tribe of the house of Rubil, he is Chief of the tribe of the house of Rubil, and he was authorized over thirty men.
11.43: Khanan, son of Maaka, Azi, who is from Nathoth.
11.44: Yushaphat who is from Astruth, Shamma and Amayel, sons of Khutham who is from Aruth.
11.45: Yadayel, son of Shamri, and Yukha his brother.
11.46: Emuzuth and Anayel, Mukham, Mutsiel, Ribay and Ashua his son, Akhmayel and Yathma, Moabites.
11.47: Eliel and Yathma and Ubir, Lasayel and Ashkir.

12.1: These are all the mighty men of David who were standing with him in battle, and these entered Tsenqlag the city with David, when he fled from before Shaul, son of Qish, and all the men of David were standing in might, and if he chose, they would have killed Shaul, son of Qish, because they were mighty men and they were men warriors, and David chose not to allow them to kill Shaul.
12.2: Because they had held bows with their left hands and swords in their right hands, and their bows were filled with arrows, and David chose not to kill Shaul, for he was Chief Prince of the tribe of the house of Benjamin.
12.3: And these are the names of the men of David who were with him: Akhiazar and Yuash his son, and Yoel who is from Maksa, Shemaiah who is from Gebath, Pelet, and Berakya, sons of Arbuth, Yahu who is from Nathoth
12.4: Shemaiah who is from Gebun, he was authorized over thirty and made war like all of them, Eramyah, Nakhzayel, Zabur, Azar
12.5: Gadi, Yarmuth, Balyah, Azri, Shamarya, Shephatiah, Khabar
12.7: Yuakh, Zechariah, the sons of Yarkhum who is from Gadar.
12.8: And men, mighty men of power and strong in stature, and valiant men from the tribe of the house of Gad, were gathered to David to go out with him for battle to Matsruth that is in the wilderness, wielders of swords and shields; their appearances were as the appearance of lions and whenever they fought on the Mountain of Herbs for him.
12.9: Azra the Chief of the Mighty Men, Oubadya the second, Eliab the third
12.10: Ashur the fourth, Eramyah the fifth
12.11: Athar the sixth, Eliel the seventh
12.12: Yokhanan the eighth, Elizabar the ninth
12.13: Eramyah the tenth, Shephatiah the eleventh.
12.14: These are from the sons of Gad, Chiefs of the army that were authorized, one over a hundred, and others were authorized each over one thousand.
12.15: These were those who crossed over the Jordan in the month of Nisan when it filled up to its banks, and they had put to flight all the army that was encamping in the plain to the East, and to the West, and these are the numbers of the Commanders of the army who came and assembled to David at Khebron to give him the kingdom of Shaul, so that the word of Samuel the Prophet would be fulfilled which he spoke from the mouth of LORD JEHOVAH.
12.16: And some sons of Benjamin and some sons of Yehuda came until they arrived at the encampment with David.
12.17: And David went out to meet them and he blessed them and said to them: “If it is for peace you have come and to help us, LORD JEHOVAH will give to you each double for the thing that is in your heart, and if it is to hinder me you have come, or to betray me to those hating me, because I have not sinned against you, neither is there a bad thing in my hands, for the God of my fathers knows, and he will rebuke him among us who does wrong against his fellow man.
12.18: And the Spirit of Power clothed himself with Amasa, son of Yathar, Chief of thirty, and he answered and said to David: “Come David, come you son of Iyshay, I am also with you! Peace to you! Do not fear, and peace is given to you from before your helper, because your God is helping you every hour!” And David received them and David appointed them Commanders of the army.
12.19: And from the tribe of the house Manassheh they went with David when he was going to make war with the Philistines, and they were not willing to go to battle with Shaul to help Shaul, because they hated him. Because of a plot they went, and they counseled with the Princes of the Philistines and they were saying to them: “We shall go first and we shall fall on Shaul our Lord when he goes to Tsnaqlag, and we will encamp against him and we shall capture him alive!”
12.20: And these are their names: Ada, Zabur, Yadil, Mankiel, Elihu and Yuzabar, Chief of the thousands of the house of Manassheh.
12.21: And those were going and they helped David when he was going against a band of robbers, because all of them were mighty men in their great might and were authorized there over all the army, and they were doing with it just as they desired.
12.22: And all day in the daytime they were entering before David to eat bread before him, because he loved them greatly.
12.24: The sons of Yehuda, the bearers of shields and those wielding spears, six thousand and eight hundred mighty men of the army.
12.25: And from the sons of Shemon, mighty men of the army for battle, eight thousand and seven hundred.
12.26: And from the sons of Levi, four thousand and six hundred.
12.27: Yawida the Prince was of the tribe of Aaron, and those with him, three thousand and seven hundred.
12.28: And Tsaduq, a young man, a mighty man of the army, and the household of his father and his Prince brothers, twenty and two.
12.29: And from the sons of Benyamin, the brothers of Shaul, a thousand three, and until the day when Shaul was killed, those were the Guards of the garrison of the house of Shaul.
12.30: And from the sons of Aphreim, twenty thousand and eight hundred men, mighty men of the army, men of fame to their families, to the household of their fathers.
12.31: And from the half tribe of Menashe, eighteen thousand men, for they were called by names, and those came first to appoint David in the Kingdom
12.32: And from the sons of Issakar, knowing wisdom in their times and workers of beautiful works and upright before LORD JEHOVAH, their two hundred Chiefs, and all their brothers, whatever they were saying to them is what they were doing.
12.33: And from the tribe of the house of Zabalaun men went out, mighty men of the army, workers of war with all their heart, and with the weapons of their armor, fifty thousand to wage war with those who were divided against the kingdom of David.
12.34: And from the sons of Naphtali, one thousand Princes, and there were men with them holding shields and spears, thirtyseven thousand.
12.35: And some of those men of the house of Dan went out, workers of war, twenty and eight thousand and six hundred.
12.36: And some men of the house of Ashir went out, mighty men of the army to wage war, forty thousand.
12.37: And from the other side of Jordan, from the tribe of the house of Rubil, and from Gad, and from half of the tribe of Menashe, as they were armed with their weapons of war, a hundred and twenty thousand.
12.38: All these men went down for battle, mighty men of the army with a true heart, and they came to Khebron to confirm David in the kingdom over all Israel, also all the Princes of Israel came with a true heart to establish David King over Israel.
12.39: And they were there with David three days, eating and drinking, because their brethren had given them their provisions.
12.40: And these are the names of the tribes that were bringing to them who were near to them: the tribe of the house of Issakar, and of the house of Zabalaun, and of the house of Naphtali; they were bringing bread with wine, and on camels, and on mules, and flour, and dried figs, and baskets of grapes, and wine, and oil, and sheep, and bulls, a great abundance, for Israel had great joy.

13.1: And David took counsel with the Princes of thousands and hundreds, and all Princes and Leaders and Rulers of Israel.
13.2: And David said to all the assembly of Israel: “If it is what is beautiful in your eyes, we will seek from LORD JEHOVAH our God that we may repair for ourselves the breach of our brethren who stand in all the cities of Israel, and we will settle Priests and Levites with them in their towns and in their hamlets, and they will be assembled and they will come to our presence
13.3: And let them pray before LORD JEHOVAH our God and inquire from him because of our sins, because we were not praying before him in days of Shaul.”
13.4: And all the assembly were saying to do so.
13.5: And David gathered all Israel from the river of Egypt and unto the entrance of Antioch, to bring the ark of LORD JEHOVAH from Quryath Narayn.
13.6: And David and all Israel came up to Quryath Narayn that is at the border of the land of the tribe of Yehuda, to bring up from there the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH God who sits above the High Cherubim, upon whom his name is called.
13.7: And they set the ark of LORD JEHOVAH in a new wagon, and they took it and they went out from the house of Abinadab, and Aza and his brothers were driving the wagon.
13.8: And David and all Israel were able before LORD JEHOVAH in strength and with songs and with harps and with tambourines and with cymbals.
13.9: And they came to the threshing floor of Ramin, and Aza reached his hand to support the Ark, because the bulls were running them to the threshing floor.
13.10: And LORD JEHOVAH was provoked against Aza, and he struck him there, because he had reached his hand upon the ark, and he died there before the ark.
13.11: And David grieved because LORD JEHOVAH had broken through a breach on Aza, and the name of that place has been called “The breach of Aza” until today.
13.12: And David was afraid of LORD JEHOVAH on that day, and he said: “How will I bring the ark of LORD JEHOVAH with me?”
13.13: And David was unwilling to bring the ark of LORD JEHOVAH with him to the city of David, and David had said to bring it to the house of UbarAdwam the Gittite.
13.14: And the ark of LORD JEHOVAH sat in the house of UbarAdwam the Gittite three months, and LORD JEHOVAH blessed the house of UbarAdwam the Gittite and everything that he had.

14.1: And Khiram, King of Tsur, sent Messengers to David, and he sent him cedar wood and Stone Masons of building, and Carpenters of wood to build him a house.
14.2: Then David had known that LORD JEHOVAH had chosen him that he would be King over Israel, and his kingdom was exalted above, that he would be King over his people Israel.
14.3: And David took wives from Jerusalem after he came from Khebron, and sons and daughters were born to David.
14.4: And these are the names of the sons that were born to him in Jerusalem: Shamua and Shakub and Nathan and Solomon.
14.5: And Yabkhar and Elishema and Eliphlat.
14.6: And Nikh and Nephag and Naphi.
14.7: And Elisha and Elida and Eliphlat.
14.8: And the Philistines heard that David was anointed to reign over all Israel, and all the Philistines had come up to seek David, and David heard and he went out to meet them.
14.9: And the Philistines came and they camped in the Valley of the Giants.
14.10: And David asked of LORD JEHOVAH and said: “Shall I go up against the Philistines, and will you give them into my hands?” And he said to him: “Go up, and I shall give them into your hands.”
14.11: And they went up to the Valley of the Gap and he destroyed them there, and David said: “LORD JEHOVAH broke through my enemies before me like a gap of waters.” Because of this, they called the name of that place Peqatha d’Turatha.
14.12: And they left their idols there, and David said to his mighty men: “Burn them in fire and scatter their ashes to the wind!”
14.13: And the Philistines continued to go up and encamp in the Valley of the Giants.
14.14: And David asked again of LORD JEHOVAH, and he said to him: “Do not go up, but turn yourselves from them and go take them from their front.”
14.15: And when you will hear the sound of wailing on the top of the mountain, then go out for battle, because then LORD JEHOVAH will have gone out before you to destroy the encampment of the Philistines.”
14.16: And David did so just as LORD JEHOVAH had commanded him, and he destroyed the encampment of the Philistines from Gebun even unto Gadar.
14.17: And David was going out to all the cities, and LORD JEHOVAH put his fear upon all the nations.

15.1: And he was building citadels for himself in the cities of David and was building a place for the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH and the implements of the Tabernacle.
15.2: Then David was telling the Levites to pick up the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH and the articles of the Tabernacle, because LORD JEHOVAH chose the Levites to serve and to carry the ark of LORD JEHOVAH and for his service for eternity.
15.3: And David gathered all Israel to Jerusalem to bring up the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH to the place that he had built for it.
15.4: And David gathered the sons of Aaron and the Levites.
15.5: And he said to them and to the sons of Qahath, to Uriel The Elder and to his brothers, a hundred and twenty.
15.6: And to the sons of Marari, Shayel The Elder and his brothers, two hundred and twenty.
15.7: To the sons of Gershun, Yuyel the Elder and his brothers, a hundred and thirty.
15.8: To the sons of Elitsaphan, Shemaiah the Elder and his brothers, a hundred and thirty.
15.9: To the sons of Khebroun, Eliab the Elder and his brothers, eighty.
15.10: To the sons of Uziel, Aminadab the Elder and his brothers, a hundred and twelve.
15.11: And David called Tsadoq the Priest, and Abiathar, and the Levites, and Uriel, and Amsa, and Yuyel, and Ashia, and Shemaiah, and Uriel and Aminadab.
15.12: And he said to them: “You are the Heads of the fathers of Levi. Be hallowed, you and your brothers, so that you will bring up the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH, God of Israel, to the place of the building that existed for it from the first
15.13: Lest LORD JEHOVAH our God would break through upon us, because we did not inquire of him about the decision.”
15.14: And the Priests and the Levites were hallowed to bring up the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH, the God of Israel.
15.15: And the Levites carried the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH, just as Moshe had commanded by the word of LORD JEHOVAH, and they carried it on their shoulders.
15.16: And David told the Elders of Levi to appoint their brothers, those who minister and sing with instruments of songs, with tambourines, and with harps, and with cymbals, that they would be heard lifting up their voices with joy.
15.17: And the Levites appointed Haman, son of Yuel, and of his brothers, Asaph, Brachyah and of the sons of Merari, their brothers, Athan, son of Qashyahu.
15.18: And with them their second brothers: Zechariah, son of Nayel, and Yukhyayel, and Eliab, and Benaya, and Asa, and Matitha, and Eliphalhu, and Meqyahu, and UbarAdom and Yuayel.
15.19: These all who were standing at the gates and singing: Haman, and Asaph and Ethan.
15.20: These all sang with hymns of praise, to minister with instruments of brass: Azarayah, Azyayel, and Yukhiyel, and Yani, and Eliab, and Masyah and Benaiah.
15.21: And those who sang with hymns of praise: Matitha, and Meqyahu, and, UbarAdom, and Uziel, and Uzyayel; these were ministering on the harp every day in the daytime at the third hour, and at the sixth and at the ninth.
15.22: And Benaiah, Commander of the Levites, was carried every day in a hut, because the place was fashioned for him.
15.23: And Berakya and Halqana were providing for the Ark whatever was needed for it.
15.24: And Shebna, and Yushaphat, and Nathaniel, and Amshi, and Zechariah, and Benaiah and Eliazar, these are the Priests who were sounding on the horns before the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH, and UbarAdom and Akhia were looking after the Ark.
15.25: And it was David and the Elders of Israel and Princes of thousands who were going to bring up the Ark of The Covenant of LORD JEHOVAH from the house of UbarAdum to the city of David with great joy.
15.26: And it was that when LORD JEHOVAH helped the Levites who bear the Ark that had the Covenant of LORD JEHOVAH in it, they sacrificed seven oxen and seven rams.
15.27: And David was clothed with a robe of fine white linen, and all the Levites who bore the Ark were wearing also robes of fine white linen, and on top of David’s robe of fine white linen, he was clothed with an ephod of fine white linen.
15.28: And David and all the house of Israel were going up to the Ark that has the Covenant of LORD JEHOVAH in it, with hymns of praise and with the sounding of straight and curved trumpets, and raising their sound unto Heaven.
15.29: And it was that when the ark that has in it the Covenant of LORD JEHOVAH had arrived and entered and arrived in its place at the city of David, Malkal, daughter of Shaul, looked out from a window, and she saw King David who was performing, and she despised him in her heart.

16.1: And they brought the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH and they placed it in its place inside the Tabernacle that David had pitched for it, and they offered sacrifices and peace offerings before the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH.
16.2: And when David had finished bringing sacrifices and peace offerings before the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH, he blessed the people of Israel in the Name of LORD JEHOVAH of Hosts.
16.3: And he distributed to all the people, all of the house of Israel, from the men and unto the women, and from young men and unto those suckling the breasts, to every man one loaf of bread and one portion, and one fine white loaf, and all the people went, each man to his house.
16.4: And he gave before the Levites who serve before LORD JEHOVAH, also they commemorate and give thanks and glorify LORD JEHOVAH God of Israel.
16.5: Asaph, their Chief, the second Zechariah, and Yuayel, and Yukhyayel, and Matitha, and Eliab, and Benaya, and Asaph, and Aminadab, and Asaph, and Nakhzayel and Asaph.
16.6: These all are the Priests who sound with horns constantly before the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH.
16.7: Then David opened his mouth on that day, he and the Chiefs of all the Priests and the Levites, to praise LORD JEHOVAH for the community of Asaph and of his brothers, and these are the headings of the songs that David spoke before the ark of LORD JEHOVAH on that day.
16.8: “Give thanks to LORD JEHOVAH and call his name! Make known among the nations his works!
16.9: Praise him and give thanks to him! Relate all his exploits!
16.10: Glory in the name of his holiness! The heart of all who seek LORD JEHOVAH shall rejoice!
16.11: Seek LORD JEHOVAH and his strength! Pray before him constantly.
16.12: Remember his miracles that he did! His miracles and the judgments of his mouth!
16.13: The seed of Abraham, his Servant, and the sons of Yaqob, the chosen ones which he chose.
16.14: He is LORD JEHOVAH our God, whose laws are in all the Earth.
16.15: Remember his covenant for eternity, the word that he commanded to a thousand generations
16.16: That he swore to Abraham, and the covenant he gave to the children of Isaac, and he circumcised Yaqob for eternity
16.17: And to Israel he gave the covenant for the eternity of eternities.
16.18: And he said to them: ‘To you I give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance.
16.19: When you were men of small numbers and dwellers in it.
16.20: And you were taken captive from nation to nation, and from kingdom to another kingdom.’
16.21: He did not allow the Rulers to harm you, and he instructed Kings for your sakes.
16.22: And he said to them: ‘You shall not touch my Anointed Ones, and to my Prophets you shall not do evil.’
16.23: Praise LORD JEHOVAH, all the Earth! Hope every day in the day of his redemption!
16.24: Relate among the nations his honor.
16.25: Because LORD JEHOVAH is great and greatly praised, and he is revered above all Kings.
16.26: Because all the idols of the nations are images, and LORD JEHOVAH made the Heavens!
16.27: And glory and beauty are before him, strength and majesty in his holy place!
16.28: Give thanks to LORD JEHOVAH, generations of the Gentiles! Give thanks before LORD JEHOVAH in his honor and in his strength.
16.29: Give thanks to LORD JEHOVAH! In honor of his name bring offerings, and give thanks before him in the prayers of your mouths, and worship LORD JEHOVAH with songs of his holiness!
16.30: And all the Earth will tremble before him, and the world will be established that it will not be moved!
16.31: The Heavens will rejoice and the Earth will sing, and they will say among the nations: ‘LORD JEHOVAH reigns!’
16.32: The sea will sing in its fullness, and the fields will rejoice, and all that is in them!
16.33: Then the trees of the forest shall sing before LORD JEHOVAH, who comes to judge the Earth! He shall judge the world by justice, and he shall rebuke the nations in faithfulness.
16.34: And say: ”Give thanks to LORD JEHOVAH, because he is good, and his grace is to eternity!”
16.35: Save us, LORD JEHOVAH! Save us and gather us and bring us from among the nations to thank the name of your holiness and to glory in your praises!
16.36: Blessed is THE LORD of LORDS of Israel from eternity and unto eternity! And all the people shall say, Amen! With a mouth sweet and pure they shall praise their God!”
16.37: And he left Asaph and his brothers there before the Ark of the Covenant of LORD JEHOVAH to serve before LORD JEHOVAH constantly, each man on his day.
16.38: And UbarAdum and his brothers, sixty and eight, and UbarAdum, the younger son of Yutham, and Khasu, these all are the Guards of the outer gates.
16.39: Tsaduq the Priest and his brothers the Priests, these all are Priests who serve in great gladness before the tent of LORD JEHOVAH, which is in the town of Gebun.
16.40: To bring sacrifices to LORD JEHOVAH on the altar of burnt offering constantly at dawn and in the evening, and to do everything it is written in the law of LORD JEHOVAH, which he gave by the hand of Moshe, to teach the sons of Israel.
16.41: And these are the names of the men who stand with hymns of praise: Haman and Arithun and the rest of the righteous men, those whose names were hidden, to thank LORD JEHOVAH, because his grace is to eternity.
16.42: And those righteous men were not glorifying with instruments of music, neither with tambourines, neither with timbrels, neither with curved horns, neither with straight ones, neither with cymbals were they glorifying, but with a sweet mouth and by pure and perfect prayer, and in righteousness and in perfection to LORD JEHOVAH, God Almighty, THE LORD of Israel, THE LORD of all works.
16.43: And David dismissed the people, and the people went, each man to his house, and David returned to his house to bless the children of his household.

17.1: And it was that when David dwelt in his house, and David said to Nathan the Prophet: “See that I dwell in a house that is covered with planks of cedar, and the Ark of LORD JEHOVAH resides within a tent of hair!”
17.2: And Nathan said to David: “Everything that is in your heart, go and do, because LORD JEHOVAH is with you.”
17.3: And it was in that night and the word of LORD JEHOVAH was upon Nathan the Prophet, and he said to him:
17.4: “Go and say thus to my Servant David: thus says LORD JEHOVAH, you shall not build for me a house to dwell in it.
17.5: For I have not dwelt in a house from the day that I brought Israel up from the land of Egypt and until today, and I have been moving from tent to tent.
17.6: Behold, as long as I have been walking among all the children of Israel, have I said a word to one of the Judges of the children of Israel whom I have commanded to judge my people Israel, and have I said to them: ‘Why have you not built a house for me that is covered in cedar?’
17.7: Also now thus you shall say to my Servant David: thus says LORD JEHOVAH of Hosts: I have taken you from pastures from behind sheep to be King over Israel my people.
17.8: And I have been with you in all things wherever you have gone, and I have destroyed all your enemies from before your presence, and I have made you a great name like the name of the Rulers that are in the earth.
17.9: And I shall plant a place for my people Israel, and I shall set it and it shall dwell in its place and will not be moved again, and the sons of The Evil One will not again carry it away captive as at the first.
17.10: From the day that I made you Judge over my people Israel, and I have given you rest from all your enemies, and LORD JEHOVAH has shown you that the Kingdom is established for you until eternity
17.11: And when the days of your life shall be fulfilled, and you shall go and you shall be gathered to your fathers, I shall establish your seed after you that proceeds from your loins, and I shall establish him with his kingdom.
17.12: And he will build up a house for my name, and I shall establish the throne of his kingdom for eternity.
17.13: I shall be to him for a father, and he shall be to me for a son, and my graces will not pass away from his presence, as that which I removed from Shaul who was before you.
17.14: And I shall make him Ruler in my house and in my kingdom for the eternity of eternities, and the throne of his kingdom shall be prepared for him unto eternity.”
17.15: Concerning all these words and concerning all this vision, thus spoke Nathan the Prophet with David.
17.16: And King David came and sat down before LORD JEHOVAH and said: “What am I considered before you, LORD of LORDS, and what is my house that you brought me here?
17.17: And this is a small thing in your eyes, LORD Almighty, for you have spoken about the house of your Servant from before us, and all the children of men who do your worship with all their heart you bring forth from darkness to light, LORD of LORDS!
17.18: And what more will David your Servant boast to speak before you? The works of your Servant are open to you, LORD OF LORDS!
17.19: Because you know what is in the heart of your Servant, you have done for him all these great things to make known to your Servant.
17.20: Because this I have known, LORD of LORDS, that there is none like you, and there is no God but only you, according to everything that we have heard with our ears.
17.21: And who is there like your people Israel, one people in the earth, for you were revealed from Heaven and you have saved them! And because of them you have performed great temptation and awesome and great affliction against the Egyptians, until you brought them out from among them.
17.22: And you prepared for yourself your people Israel to be for you a people until the eternity of eternities, and you, LORD JEHOVAH, have been to them God.
17.23: Therefore, LORD JEHOVAH, the word that you spoke concerning your Servant and concerning the children of his house endures to eternity, and do just as you said.
17.24: And your Servants will be confirmed for eternity, and your name shall be glorified within the world, and they shall be saying: “LORD JEHOVAH of Hosts, THE LORD of LORDS, God of Israel!” And the house of David your Servant shall be prepared before you for eternity!
17.25: Because you, my God, have revealed the secret to your servant, and you have said to him: ‘Build a house for yourself.’ Because of this, your Servant has set in his heart to pray before you this prayer.
17.26: Therefore, LORD JEHOVAH, you are my God, and all your words are truth! Those that you have spoken to your Servant are good!
17.27: And from now on be revealed to bless the household of your Servant that it will be before you for the eternity of eternities, because you are LORD JEHOVAH, THE LORD of LORDS! You have spoken, and from your blessing the households of the righteous ones are blessed to eternity!”

18.1: And so it was after these things and David put the Philistines to the sword, and he destroyed them, and David took a cord of measurement from the hand of the Philistines, and he took Gath and its hamlets; those that were around the Philistines he broke into pieces.
18.2: And he put the sword to the Moabites, and the Moabites became Servants, the Tax Collectors.
18.3: And David killed Hadarezar, King of Netsibin, when he went to set the border in the River Euphrates.
18.4: And David seized from him one thousand double horse chariots, and seven thousand horsemen, and David overthrew all the two horse chariots and he left of them a hundred double horse chariots.
18.5: And the men of Adum and Darmsuq came to help Hadarzar, King of Netsibin, and David killed twenty and two thousand men of Adum.
18.6: And David raised up Rulers in Darmsuq, and the Damascenes became Servants to David, Tax Collectors, and LORD JEHOVAH saved David in every place that he went.
18.7: And David picked up the shields of gold that were hanging on the horses of the Servants of Hedarezar, and he brought them to Jerusalem.
18.8: And from Tebakh and from Bruthi, cities of Hedarezar, David took very much brass, and from it David made the sea of brass and the pillars of brass and bulls of brass and the implements of brass, a great abundance.
18.9: And Pul, King of the Antiochenes, heard that David put the armies of Hedarezar King of Netsibin to the sword.
18.10: And he sent Yuram, his son, to King David, to inquire of his welfare and to congratulate him, because he fought with Hedarezar, and he killed him, because Hedarezar was a man warrior, and in the hand of Yuram there were vessels of silver and vessels of gold and vessels of brass.
18.11: Also some of them King David hallowed to LORD JEHOVAH with silver and gold that he had taken from all the nations that he had defeated, of the Edomites, and of the sons of Amon, and of the Moabites, and of the Philistines and of the Amalekites.
18.12: And Abishi, son of Tsuriya, the brother of Yuab, put the Edomites to the sword in the Valley of Salt, eighteen thousand men.
18.13: And David appointed Governors over the Edomites, and all the Edomites were Servants to David, and LORD JEHOVAH saved David in every place that he was going.
18.14: And David reigned over all Israel, and David was executing judgment and righteousness for all the people.
18.15: And Yuab, son of Tsuriya, was authorized over all the armies, and Yahushaphat son of Akhilud was the Governor.
18.16: And Tsaduq, son of Akhitub, and Akhimlek, son of Abiathar the Priest, and Sariya the Scribe
18.17: And Benaya, son of Yuyada, was over the Archers and over the Slingers, and the sons of David were Princes.

19.1: And it happened after these things, and Nakhash King of the children of Amun died, and Khanun his son reigned after him.
19.2: And David said: “I shall do a kindness with Khanun, son of Nakhash, just as his father did a kindness with me.” And David sent Messengers to comfort him concerning his father, and the Servants of David came to the land of the children Amon to Khanun to comfort him concerning his father.
19.3: And the Princes of the children of Amon were saying to Khanun their Lord: “Surely David was honoring your father for his life because, look, he sent comforters to you to spy out the city, and to know the exits and the entrances David sent his Servants to us!”
19.4: And Khanun took the Servants of David, and he shaved the half of their beards and the half of their heads, and he cut off their tunics unto their navels, and he sent them away.
19.5: And they told David, and he sent some men to meet them, because they were very ashamed, and the King said to them: “Stay in Jericho until whenever your beards will grow, and you shall return.”
19.6: And the sons of Amon saw that they had done wrong against the Servants of David, and Khanun and the sons of Amon sent to hire for themselves with a thousand talents of silver from Aram Nahrayn, and from Kharan, and from Netsibin, and from Adum, double horse chariots and horsemen.
19.7: And they hired for themselves thirty two thousand horsemen, and the King of Kharan, and the King of Adum, and the King of Aram Nahrayn, and the King of Netsibin and their armies, and they came and they encamped before Medeba, and the sons of Amon were assembled from their cities and they came to the battle.
19.8: And David heard and he sent Yuab and all the armies of mighty men.
19.9: And the sons of Amon went out and made war before the gate, and those Kings and their armies camping by themselves in the fields.
19.10: And Yuab saw that the battle was intense upon him from before him and from behind him, and he chose to himself from all the mighty men of Israel, and they made war against Adum.
19.11: And the rest of the people he handed over to Abishi his brother, and he arrayed against the sons of Amon
19.12: And he said to him: “If it is that Adum will be stronger than I, you will come, you will save me, and if the sons of Amon will be stronger than you, I will come, and I will save you from them!
19.13: Be strong and be valiant, and we shall fight for the sake of our people, and for the sake of the cities of our God, and LORD JEHOVAH shall do what is beautiful in his eyes!”
19.14: And Yuab came and the people that were with him to fight with the Edomites, and they fled from before him.
19.15: And the sons of Amon saw that the Edomites had fled, and they fled also from before Abishi his brother, and they came to the city and Yuab returned to Jerusalem.
19.16: And the Edomites saw that they were defeated before the sons of Israel, and they sent Messengers, and they brought out the Edomites that were from the crossing of the river, and they came to Khilam, and Shubak, General of the army of Hedarezar, was in front of them.
19.17: And they told David, and he gathered all Israel, and he came to them and David arrayed against the Edomites; they fought war with them.
19.18: And the Edomites fled from before the sons of Israel, and David put to the sword among the Edomites seven thousand double horse chariots, and four thousand footmen, and Shubak, General of the army of Hedarezar, was killed there.
19.19: And the Servants of Hedarezar saw that they were defeated before the sons of Israel, and they made peace with David, and they served him, and the Edomites did not again choose to help the sons of Amon.

20.1: And it was the next year at the time of the going forth of Kings, and Yuab gathered the armies and he came and he encamped against the land of the sons of Amon, and he seized on it and he encamped against Rabbath their great city, and David was dwelling in Jerusalem, and Yuab seized Rabbath the city of the children of Amon, and he destroyed it.
20.2: And David took the crown of their King from his head, and he weighed it, and he found in it the weight of a talent of gold, and there were fine gems in it, and David placed it on his head, and he brought out very much loot of the city.
